Chapter 1 Sales Data Analysis (2015–2020) 1.1 Learning Outcomes After completing this experiment, students will be able to: - Enter and validate data in Excel - Apply formulas (SUM, AVERAGE) - Create and format charts (Line/Column) - Export results to Word - Perform Sorting and Advanced Filtering 1.2 Dataset (As per Excel File) Year Product Region Sales 2015 A South 50000 2016 A South 60000 2017 A South 65000 2018 A South 70000 2019 A South 80000 2020 A South 90000 1.2.1  Download Excel File Download Sales Data Excel 1.3 Step 1: Data Entry & Formulas (Based on Excel File) 1.3.1 Procedure Open Microsoft Excel → New Workbook Enter the dataset in columns A–D In column D, enter Sales values Leave column E for calculations 1.3.2 Formulas (Already Embedded in Excel File) Cell Description Formula E9 Total Sales =SUM(D2:D7) E10 Average Sales =AVERAGE(D2:D7) 1.3.3 Labels in Excel D9 → “Total” D10 → “Average”  This structure is already implemented in the provided Excel file 1.4 Step 2: Chart Creation 1.4.1 Procedure Select range A1:D7 Insert → Charts Choose: Line Chart (Trend Analysis) Column Chart (Comparison) 1.4.2 Diagram: Chart Creation Flow flowchart LR A[Select Data] --> B[Insert Tab] B --> C[Choose Chart Type] C --> D[Line Chart] C --> E[Column Chart] 1.4.3 Expected Output Sales increase steadily from 2015 to 2020 1.5 Step 3: Chart Formatting 1.5.1 Procedure Add Chart Title: Sales Trend (2015–2020) Add Axis Titles (Year, Sales) Add Legend Apply styles 1.5.2 Diagram: Chart Elements graph TD A[Chart] --> B[Title] A --> C[X Axis] A --> D[Y Axis] A --> E[Legend] 1.6 Step 4: Export to Word 1.6.1 Procedure Copy Excel table → Paste in Word Copy chart → Paste in Word 1.6.2 Interpretation The sales data shows a consistent upward trend from 2015 to 2020. The increase in total and average sales indicates steady business growth. 1.7 Step 5: Sorting 1.7.1 Procedure Select dataset (A1:D7) Data → Sort Apply: Year → Ascending Sales → Descending 1.7.2 Diagram flowchart TD A[Dataset] --> B[Sort] B --> C[Ascending] B --> D[Descending] 1.8  Step 6: Advanced Filter 1.8.1 Procedure Create criteria range: Year 2018 Go to Data → Advanced Filter Select: List Range → A1:D7 Criteria Range → Criteria Table Apply filter 1.8.2 Diagram flowchart LR A[Full Data] --> B[Apply Criteria] B --> C[Filtered Output] 1.9 Final Output Excel file with embedded formulas (E9, E10) Line/Column chart Word document with interpretation 1.10 Conclusion This experiment demonstrates how Excel tools can be used effectively for structured data analysis, visualization, and decision-making using real formulas embedded in the worksheet.
